We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:

Regularly and properly prepare the BRS of all bank accounts, showing all the reconciling items identified, both in the book and bank balances, to bring back the cash balances to their correct amounts, and submit the same to the Auditor's Office within the prescribed period; and 

Prepare the necessary adjusting/ correcting journal entries and submit the JEV taking up subject adjustments to the Audit Team. Adherence to the provision of COA Circular 96-011 and Section 74 of PD 1445 is required.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to instruct the:

Inventory Committee to prioritize the completion of the physical count of PPEs and prepare the RPCPPE, reconcile the same with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records; and

Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE, Budget Officer, Accountant, and Treasurer (Local Finance Committee):

Stop the practice of implementing prior periods’ priority projects without cash backup; 

Exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of government funds; and,

Monitor the availability of the budget and the cash each month and to prioritize expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed to settle all their accounts with the GSIS to prevent incurring further interest for late payments and avoid adverse effects on the employees. 

We further recommended that the management prioritize the payments of GSIS premiums to avoid penalties and interest.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF deposited in the General Fund bank account to the Special Trust Fund account. Also, include in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the unexpended balance of the fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Submit an explanation or justification why rental cost of heavy equipment was included in the Program of Work and Detailed Estimates of the listed projects implemented by administration;

We recommended and the management agreed that they require the Inventory Committee:

To conduct an inventory of all PPE accounts once every year and COA must be invited as an observer;

The disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs should be strictly implemented in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

Request from COA an authority to derecognize the unaccountable missing PPE from the books of accounts after an investigation in accordance with Paragraph 8 of this Circular.

We recommended and the management agreed that they direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of Bail Bonds Payable to the Trust Fund account under Guaranty/Security Deposit Payable. 

Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant exerts extra effort to locate the documents and other records to determine the claimants and the corresponding amount, otherwise, revert those undocumented accounts to an unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended and the management agreed that they:

Submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002, certified correct by the Local Accountant. Please include the reference documents such as the DV No. or Check No. of the transaction for us to be able to verify the transaction.

Moving forward, the LDRRMO shall submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA and Disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements. (A Sample of disclosure is in Annex D of COA circular 2012-002).;

The unutilized balance of the LDRRMF shall be available for use in disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP within the next five years. Any unutilized amount after five years shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian. Illustrative accounting entries are shown in Annex C; and

Municipal Accountant to establish the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM and other related accounts and make the necessary adjustments.

We recommended and the management agreed that they directs the Budget Officer, Accountant, and Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of government funds. They should limit the expenditures within the total income realized during the year to avoid the incurrence of cash overdrafts and find ways to cover the cash deficit with the collections in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the management agreed that they:

Direct the Municipal Accountant prepare regularly the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts and submits the same to the Auditor's Office within the prescribed period; and 

Management to authorize the Municipal Accountant to have online access to retrieve the bank statements quickly and conveniently.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records in order to come up with more reliable costing of the PPE accounts; and, 

Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to adjust/correct the affected accounts if necessary.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be directed to submit the paid disbursement vouchers, including supporting documents, to the Audit Team for examination and verification purposes in accordance with existing laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the responsible Municipal Officials to immediately submit the pertinent documents of the above projects to facilitate the technical review and inspections, and moving forward, strictly comply with the above-mentioned regulations. 

Also, the Management to identify the cause of non-submission and take necessary action to solve the issue.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately pay the amount of P888, 412.83 as per demand letter of the GSIS dated January 31, 2022 and to remit monthly premium contributions and other amounts due to the GSIS within the reglementary period as required under R.A. No. 8291 to avoid the imposition of penalties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: 

to remit the balance immediately as of December 31, 2020, to the concerned agency; and 

strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of the premiums, contributions and loan amortizations of the officers and employees of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: 

to remit the balance immediately as of December 31, 2020, to the concerned agency; and 

strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of the premiums, contributions and loan amortizations of the officers and employees of the Municipality.

We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) prepare the Report on Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System with the Inventory Committee's help. 

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and their components, reconcile it with the result of the physical count at year-end, correctly depreciate the accounts, and disclose it in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to perform the following:

Strictly enforce the liquidation of all cash advances within thirty (30) days upon return to the permanent official station; 

A demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2023, from concerned officials and employees, including the suspension of salaries; and 

Strictly adhere to applicable rules and regulations in the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the budgetary limitations as provided for under Sec. 331(b) of RA 7160. The management would consider the implications of their decisions relative to PS which could increase the cost of personal services; and institute strong internal control over their transactions to ensure that the LGU has a balanced budget and that funds are allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We reiterate our recommendation for the Local Chief Executive and the Local Finance Committee (LFC), (Budget Officer, Accountant, and the Treasurer) to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.
Also, to ensure that the incurrence of expenditures is within the total income realized during the year and to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft, the LFC to:
Monitor the availability of the budget and the cash each month and to prioritize expenditures;
Prepare a cash budget to determine the probable collections that can be reasonably collected for a certain period of time and to itemize the necessary expenditures; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to improve the collection especially on RPT and LEEs and all departments/offices to minimize expenses to improve financial condition of the municipality.

Recommendation
We recommend that management to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the paid disbursement vouchers including its supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification purpose and in compliance with the above-cited regulation. Otherwise, the responsible officials may be liable for failure to render account punishable under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit all paid DVs together with its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the above-cited laws, rules and regulations. Otherwise, the responsible officials may be liable for failure to render account punishable under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office in coordination with the General Service Office to identify the composition of the items presented as Various Expenses and Office Supplies and Materials and prepare the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to have the transactions be recorded in the appropriate accounts.
Also, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to derecognize the PPE items that were already sold and transferred to other government agencies.

Recommendation
We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the proper account codes as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU) to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. Also, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to correct the classification of the PPE items.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned officers to comply with the requirements of the above-mentioned circular in accounting and maintaining of records and reporting of the road networks of the local government.

Recommendation
We reiterate our recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the fund to Trust Fund.
Also, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Review the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) made in the recording of transactions pertaining to this fund; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting JEV to correct the error/s made in recording and properly book-up the liability in the books of account.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
To require the contractor and the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation/justification for the delays incurred in the completion of the Phase I project;
To require the immediate completion of the project (Phase I) to facilitate the implementation of the Phase II;
To impose liquidated damages on the contractor equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of works for every day of delay pursuant to Item 8.1 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
To adopt measures and strategies to fast track the implementation of projects.

Recommendation
We recommend that management:
Ensure that the necessary documents are prepared and duly approved before project implementation;
Require the MEO to ensure that the POW and detailed estimates are based on a duly approved plan to ensure the reasonableness of the project cost; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the paid disbursement vouchers including its supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification purposes.

Recommendation
We reiterate our recommendation for the management to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulation in appropriating and utilizing the 20% EDF. Also, to realign the remaining unimplemented maintenance and operating expenditures to projects which are allowable to be funded from the 20% EDF.

Recommendation
We recommend that the management to prioritize the completion of the necessary amenities of the local government disaster recovery facility to ensure efficient and effective response in times of calamity. Also, for management to coordinate with the other identified facilities for these to be provided with the necessary amenities.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

Recommendation
We recommend that management to:
Conduct thorough evaluation and review of the municipality’s need for hiring casuals, contractual and JO employees;
Ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC resolutions to ensure protection of government funds from unnecessary expenses; and
Require the HRMO to evaluate the staffing pattern of the Municipality in order to limit the hiring of casual, contractual and job order employees and be guided by the provisions of the CSC resolutions.

Recommendation
We recommend that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. The Monthly reports will provide a summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order based on the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

Recommendation
We recommend that management to comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines, and reporting and monitoring.

Recommendation
We recommend that Management to comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

Recommendation
We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities to attain gender equality, monitor its implementation and report on its results.

Recommendation
We recommend that management to review the payments for the procurement of goods and services, and infrastructure contracts in CY 2018 and compute for the tax due and settle with the BIR. Also, moving forward, the management to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes as required in Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

Recommendation
We recommend that Management to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas for the coverage of government hired JOs and PBCs under the program. Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the General Service Officer and Municipal Engineer, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and purchase orders including Inspection and Acceptance Reports as provided for in COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the recommendation upon demand.
observations.reason: Copies of contract submitted upon demand and the Pos still not submitted to the Team.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Engineer to display notification so that the public will be properly informed in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Some of the LGU Projects with the required billboard notification.
observations.reason: Not all the PPAs are provided with the required notifications

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC of the Municipality implement the standard rate on the sale of bidding documents as prescribed under GPPB Resolution No 04-2012.
observations.managementAction: Sale of bid documents at standard rates

observations.recommendation:
Stop operating an open dumpsite for its garbage disposal instead sanitary landfill should be developed as final garbage disposal site for the LGU.
Management should give priority attention in the preparation and approval of its Local Government Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as required under RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Open dumpsite has been closed; Submitted the Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan to Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) but returned with findings.
observations.reason: Solid Waste Management Plan is not yet approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Committee should identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.
observations.managementAction: Implemented all the 13 projects

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should issue instructions that the project be made available to the public particularly the vendors so that these can be utilized for its intended purpose.
Management should facilitate the submission of municipal ordinance to the Sanggunian Bayan for its approval to fast track its immediate implementation and usage of the new project.
observations.managementAction: Part of the Pasalubong center was occupied by the IPs with no rental fees for 6 months.
observations.reason: The entire Pasalubong Center still not utilized and some areas were already damaged.

observations.recommendation: Management should instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification purpose and in compliance with the above cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: Submit DVs

observations.recommendation: Management is required to submit the supporting documents in order to check the propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Submit DVs

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with the rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes from compensation and other sources, intact, as required under BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012.
observations.managementAction: Remitted the amount withheld
observations.reason: Taxes withheld on goods not remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the payment of procured items should be made directly to the supplier/dealer and stop the practice of paying procured supplies and materials thru reimbursements and the appropriate procurement procedure shall be strictly adhered to.  Furthermore, all the required documents shall be complied and complete to ensure that the approval of these disbursement vouchers should first be sought before payments are made.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement were minimized
observations.reason: Reimbursements were accommodated only to some officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all government vehicles be marked with the name of the Municipality and “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The LGU vehicles are marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all collections should be deposited intact daily to the Liquidating Officer and/or authorized depository bank strictly in accordance with the provision cited under Section 32 of COA Circular 2002-003 and Joint Circular 1-81 of DOF and COA.
observations.managementAction: All the collectors deposit their collections every week.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should remit the amount ofP6,796,934.37 to authorized collecting agent to avoid payment of penalties and surcharges due to its late remittance
observations.managementAction: Partial remittance
observations.reason: The balance of the inter-agency payables on mandatory remittance were decreased.
